title: Sphaeralcea rivularis
intro: Sphaeralcea rivularis, also known as the desert marigold, is a perennial wildflower native to the southwestern United States and Mexico, characterized by its bright yellow and orange flowers. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which may help to soothe digestive issues and reduce the risk of infections. The bioactive constituents of Sphaeralcea rivularis include flavonoids, carotenoids, and saponins, which are thought to contribute to its medicinal properties. In herbal medicine, Sphaeralcea rivularis is often prepared as a tincture, tea, or dried flower extract, which may be used to support immune function and promote overall well-being.
taxonomy: Sphaeralcea rivularis is a species of flowering plant in the mallow family, Malvaceae. It is native to western North America and is also known as the desert marigold. The plant is classified as a perennial herb with bright orange to yellow flowers. The species name "rivularis" refers to the plant's habitat near rivers and streams. This classification helps identify the plant's unique characteristics and its place within the larger botanical family.
